findings.justification: ajax_paginate() is reachable by any authenticated user via wp_ajax_um_ajax_paginate and performs only a nonce check (UM()->check_ajax_nonce()), with no current_user_can() capability check, allowing any subscriber-level user to trigger arbitrary um_ajax_load_posts__* hook actions.

The raw $_REQUEST['args'] array is passed without any sanitization or validation directly to do_action("um_ajax_load_posts__{$hook}", $args), meaning all hook handlers receive attacker-controlled input that may be misused by downstream handlers.

The buffered output of hook handlers is returned via die($output) with no HTML escaping or wp_kses filtering, so any hook handler that echoes user-controlled data can produce a stored or reflected XSS payload delivered to the caller.
